claim: The three-cipher pulse (top action / debt / context) shall be structurally present in the LIVE-STATE block of the root CLAUDE.md, regenerated on every gen-spec / land, so the operator's per-turn ORIENT step reads it by reference rather than re-deriving it.
why: Closes the per-turn edge of the inversion. The three-cipher pulse (top action / debt / context) is required by the boot ritual to be cited in the first sentence of every substantive reply (R-boot-cite-in-first-sentence). In the Go port the pulse IS structurally generated: BuildLiveState (internal/generator/livestate.go) reads diagnose.DiagnoseSignals for the top-action line, computes the SETTLED/DRAFT/OPEN/closeable-debt counts for the debt line, and emits the context line (ctxLineStatic — the honest UNMEASURED explanation) verbatim; this runs on every `hotam gen-spec` and every `hotam land` (cmd/hotam/land.go step b → genSpec → BuildLiveState). The Mediation loop step 1 (ORIENT, embedded in CLAUDE.md) instructs the operator to reload this pulse by reference. NOT YET PORTED is the automatic per-turn HOOK INJECTION: the historical emit_cipher.py (a UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.local.json) read the LIVE-STATE sentinels and emitted a JSON hookSpecificOutput consumed by the host harness, structurally forcing the pulse into every user turn. emit-cipher is Declared (not ported) in methodology.Tools, so in the Go port the pulse is present in LIVE-STATE but the operator reads it manually per the ORIENT step, not via an auto-injected hook. The original WHY stands: structural presence of the pulse prevents the operator from forgetting to orient. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer test exists in the Go port (the historical test_user_prompt_submit_hook_emits_cipher was Python-only).
